message:
test(hook),fix(patterns): pin hook regression contract + close dedup gaps

Two fresh-eyes follow-ups on yesterday's #2/#3/#4/#5 fixes:

1. internal/cli/hook_test.go — new regression tests for issues
   #3, #4, and #5 against the actual `slb hook generate` output.
   Pinned the source-level shapes that were silently broken:

   - r'^rm\s+-[rf]{2}' MUST appear (no `\\s` doubling) — issue #4.
   - p.search MUST be used (no anchored p.match) — #4 follow-on.
   - hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision MUST be emitted; the
     legacy `{"action": "block"}` shape MUST NOT — issue #5.
   - _project_root_for_socket helper MUST exist and walk up
     looking for .slb/ — issue #3.
   - _emit_decision MUST default unknown actions to "ask"
     (fail-closed), not "allow" (the prior shape that made the
     daemon RPC fail open if it ever returned a future verdict).

   Without these tests, future code review or accidental edits
   could re-introduce any of the four bugs and ship them.

2. internal/cli/patterns.go::loadCustomPatternsIntoDefaultEngine —
   two real holes in the dedup logic that yesterday's idempotency
   pass missed:

   a. The dedup key used `row.Tier` directly, but parseTier is
      case-insensitive ("Dangerous" → "dangerous"). A row inserted
      via direct SQL with mixed-case tier would never match the
      engine's lowercase-keyed `AllPatterns()` map, so dedup
      would silently fail. Switched to the canonical
      `string(tier)` from parseTier.

   b. The loader didn't add successfully-loaded patterns back
      into the dedup set, so two duplicate rows in the same DB
      (e.g. a corpus migrated from before the UNIQUE constraint
      existed) would both populate the engine. Now mark loaded
      after each successful AddPattern.

All slb tests pass: 7 cli pattern tests (incl. 1 new), 2 new hook
generate-shape tests, plus the existing daemon, db, core suites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
